title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: vignette reproduces real-data analysis paper using afpca package. apply Adaptive Functional Principal Components Analysis (Adaptive FPCA) trial-averaged neural firing rate data recorded reaching task, compare results standard fpca.sc method refund package. dataset (firing_rates_data) ships package contains trial-averaged mean activation profiles (spikes/sec) 25 neurons 1.74-second window centered around movement cue.

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: load required packages firing rates dataset included package.
code: library(afpca) library(refund) library(tidyverse) library(tidyfun) library(patchwork)  data(firing_rates_data)

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: firing_rates_data object tibble functional data format, row corresponds one neuron activation column stores mean firing rate profile tfd (tidy functional data) object. time index shifted t=0t = 0 corresponds cue onset. plot shows raw activation profiles neurons. x-axis represents time relative movement cue (seconds), y-axis represents mean activation spikes per second. package contain neuron-averages displayed Panel B1 Figure 1.
code: firing_rates_data %>%   tf_unnest(activation) %>%   mutate(activation_arg = activation_arg - 0.25) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = activation_arg, y = activation_value)) +   geom_line(aes(group = neuron_no), linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.5) +   theme_bw() +   labs(     x = "Time from cue (sec)",     y = "Mean Activation (spikes / sec)"   ) +   theme(text = element_text(size = 12),         legend.position = "none")

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: Figure 4 compares two methods across two panels: Panel displays first two estimated functional principal components (FPCs) model. FPC shown mean function (black) along perturbations 25th (red) 75th (blue) percentiles corresponding scores, illustrating direction magnitude activation pattern. Panel B shows observed vs. reconstructed activation profiles four representative neurons, allowing visual assessment reconstruction quality method.

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: first extract functional data standard numeric matrix Y.data, rows neurons columns timepoints. format expected fpca.adapt fpca.sc. fit two models: Adaptive FPCA (fpca.adapt): method proposed paper, uses adaptive spline basis capture spatially heterogeneous smoothness FPCs. fpca.sc (refund::fpca.sc): standard FPCA via penalized splines, used benchmark. FPC signs flipped fitting interpretability, dominant pattern corresponds increased activation following cue.
code: Y.data <- firing_rates_data %>%   tf_spread(activation) %>%   select(-neuron_no) %>%   as.matrix()  # Adaptive FPCA Fit.Adaptive.FPCA <- fpca.adapt(data = t(Y.data), nbs = 40, n.comp = 15) Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs   <- Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs   * -1 Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores <- Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores * -1  # Standard FPCA (fpca.sc) Fit.sc <- fpca.sc(Y = Y.data, nbasis = 5) Fit.sc$efunctions <- Fit.sc$efunctions * -1 Fit.sc$scores     <- Fit.sc$scores     * -1

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
code: time_grid <- 1:174 / 100 - 0.25  # Helper: build a data frame for one FPC from one model make_fpc_df <- function(mu, phi, scores, fpc_no, model) {   data.frame(     Mu     = mu,     plus   = mu + phi * quantile(scores, 0.75),     minus  = mu + phi * quantile(scores, 0.25),     time   = time_grid,     fpc_no = fpc_no,     model  = model   ) }  fpcs_data <- bind_rows(   make_fpc_df(Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$mean, Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs[, 1],               Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores[, 1], "Activation Pattern 1", "Adaptive FPCA"),   make_fpc_df(Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$mean, Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs[, 2],               Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores[, 2], "Activation Pattern 2", "Adaptive FPCA"),   make_fpc_df(Fit.sc$mu, Fit.sc$efunctions[, 1],               Fit.sc$scores[, 1], "Activation Pattern 1", "fpca.sc"),   make_fpc_df(Fit.sc$mu, Fit.sc$efunctions[, 2],               Fit.sc$scores[, 2], "Activation Pattern 2", "fpca.sc") )  figure4_panelA <- fpcs_data %>%   ggplot(aes(x = time)) +   geom_line(aes(y = Mu),    colour = "black") +   geom_line(aes(y = plus),  colour = "blue") +   geom_line(aes(y = minus), colour = "red") +   facet_grid(model ~ fpc_no) +   labs(x = "Time from cue (sec)",        y = "Mean Activation\n(spikes / sec)") +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11))  figure4_panelA

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
code: # Helper: build a data frame for one neuron from one model make_neuron_df <- function(idx, label, model_name, observed_mat, fitted_mat) {   data.frame(     observed = observed_mat[, idx],     fitted   = fitted_mat[, idx],     time     = time_grid,     model    = model_name,     neuron   = label   ) }  neuron_idx    <- c(1, 15, 25, 4) neuron_labels <- paste("Neuron", 1:4)  neurons_data <- bind_rows(   Map(make_neuron_df, neuron_idx, neuron_labels,       "Adaptive FPCA", list(t(Y.data)), list(Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$Y_hat)),   Map(make_neuron_df, neuron_idx, neuron_labels,       "fpca.sc", list(t(Y.data)), list(t(Fit.sc$Yhat))) ) %>% bind_rows()  figure4_panelB <- neurons_data %>%   ggplot(aes(x = time)) +   geom_line(aes(y = observed, colour = "Observed"),      alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(aes(y = fitted,   colour = "Reconstructed")) +   facet_grid(model ~ neuron) +   labs(x = "Time from cue (sec)",        y = "Mean Activation\n(spikes / sec)") +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Observed" = "purple", "Reconstructed" = "black")   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11), legend.title = element_blank())  figure4_panelB

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
code: figure4_panelA + figure4_panelB +   plot_layout(widths = c(2, 4)) &   theme(text = element_text(size = 11))

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: vignette reproduces real-data analysis paper “Functional Principal Components Analysis Locally Adaptive Smoothing” using afpca package. apply Adaptive Functional Principal Components Analysis (Adaptive FPCA) trial-averaged neural firing rate data recorded reaching task, compare results standard fpca.sc method refund package.

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
code: library(afpca) library(refund) require(tidyverse) require(tidyfun) require(patchwork)  data(firing_rates_data)

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: firing_rates_data contains trial-averaged mean activation profiles (spikes/sec) 25 neurons 1.74-second window. row one neuron, activation column stores firing rate profile tfd functional data object. time axis shifted t=0t = 0 corresponds cue onset. neuron-level averages displayed Panel B1 Figure 1 paper.
code: firing_rates_data %>%   tf_unnest(activation) %>%   mutate(activation_arg = activation_arg - 0.25) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = activation_arg, y = activation_value, group = neuron_no)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.5) +   labs(x = "Time from cue (sec)", y = "Mean Activation (spikes / sec)") +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 12))

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: Figure 4 compares Adaptive FPCA fpca.sc across two panels: Panel shows first two estimated FPCs model, displayed estimated mean function (black) perturbed 25th (red) 75th (blue) percentiles corresponding scores. Panel B shows observed vs. reconstructed activation profiles four representative neurons.

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: reshape functional data numeric matrix (neurons × timepoints), fit models, flip FPC signs dominant pattern reflects increased post-cue activation.
code: Y.data <- firing_rates_data %>%   tf_spread(activation) %>%   select(-neuron_no) %>%   as.matrix()  # Adaptive FPCA Fit.Adaptive.FPCA <- fpca.adapt(data = t(Y.data), nbs = 40, n.comp = 15) Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs   <- Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs   * -1 Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores <- Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores * -1  # Standard FPCA Fit.sc <- fpca.sc(Y = Y.data, nbasis = 5) Fit.sc$efunctions <- Fit.sc$efunctions * -1 Fit.sc$scores     <- Fit.sc$scores     * -1

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: helper make_fpc_df() builds tidy data frame single FPC, storing mean mean +/- FPC.
code: time_grid <- 1:174 / 100 - 0.25  make_fpc_df <- function(mu, phi, scores, fpc_no, model) {   data.frame(     Mu    = mu,     plus  = mu + phi * quantile(scores, 0.75),     minus = mu + phi * quantile(scores, 0.25),     time  = time_grid,     fpc_no = fpc_no,     model  = model   ) }  fpcs_data <- bind_rows(   make_fpc_df(Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$mean, Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs[, 1],               Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores[, 1], "Activation Pattern 1", "Adaptive FPCA"),   make_fpc_df(Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$mean, Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs[, 2],               Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores[, 2], "Activation Pattern 2", "Adaptive FPCA"),   make_fpc_df(Fit.sc$mu, Fit.sc$efunctions[, 1],               Fit.sc$scores[, 1], "Activation Pattern 1", "fpca.sc"),   make_fpc_df(Fit.sc$mu, Fit.sc$efunctions[, 2],               Fit.sc$scores[, 2], "Activation Pattern 2", "fpca.sc") )  figure4_panelA <- fpcs_data %>%   ggplot(aes(x = time)) +   geom_line(aes(y = Mu),    colour = "black") +   geom_line(aes(y = plus),  colour = "blue") +   geom_line(aes(y = minus), colour = "red") +   facet_grid(model ~ fpc_no) +   labs(x = "Time from cue (sec)", y = "Mean Activation\n(spikes / sec)") +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11))  figure4_panelA

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
text: helper make_neuron_df() pairs observed model-reconstructed profiles single neuron. four representative neurons model.
code: make_neuron_df <- function(idx, label, model_name, observed_mat, fitted_mat) {   data.frame(     observed = observed_mat[, idx],     fitted   = fitted_mat[, idx],     time     = time_grid,     model    = model_name,     neuron   = label   ) }  neuron_idx    <- c(1, 4, 15, 25) neuron_labels <- paste("Neuron", 1:4)  neurons_data <- bind_rows(   Map(make_neuron_df, neuron_idx, neuron_labels,       "Adaptive FPCA", list(t(Y.data)), list(Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$Y_hat)),   Map(make_neuron_df, neuron_idx, neuron_labels,       "fpca.sc",       list(t(Y.data)), list(t(Fit.sc$Yhat))) ) %>% bind_rows()  figure4_panelB <- neurons_data %>%   ggplot(aes(x = time)) +   geom_line(aes(y = observed, colour = "Observed"),      alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(aes(y = fitted,   colour = "Reconstructed")) +   facet_grid(model ~ neuron) +   labs(x = "Time from cue (sec)", y = "Mean Activation\n(spikes / sec)") +   scale_color_manual(values = c("Observed" = "purple", "Reconstructed" = "black")) +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11), legend.title = element_blank())  figure4_panelB

title: Real Data Analysis Reproducibility
code: figure4_panelA + figure4_panelB +   plot_layout(widths = c(2, 4)) &   theme(text = element_text(size = 11))

code: @Manual{,   title = {afpca: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis},   author = {Angel {Garcia de la Garza} and Jeff Goldsmith and Britton Sauerbrei},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9},   url = {https://github.com/angelgar/afpca}, }

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis Authors: Angel Garcia de la Garza, Britton Sauerbrei, Jeff Goldsmith License: MIT. See LICENSE file details Version: 0.9

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: afpca implements Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis (aFPCA), method estimating directions variation functional data exhibit sharp changes smoothness. Standard FPCA methods impose global smoothness assumption can fail capture abrupt transitions underlying signal. afpca addresses combining fast scalable adaptive scatterplot smoothing technique probabilistic FPCA framework, allowing functional principal components smoothed adaptively. particularly useful applications neural recordings, sharp changes activity following stimulus must distinguished smooth baseline behavior.

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: can install development version afpca GitHub :
code: install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("angelgar/afpca")

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: examples running adaptive FPCA. details use package can found XYZ. code uses function afpca::simulate_adaptive_functional_data() simulate 20 curves Yi(t)Y_i(t), =1,…,20i = 1,\dots,20 observed 200 time points common grid domain (0,1) gaussian noise. functions generated mean function two functional principal components varying temporal smoothness defined : μ(t)=t−3/2\mu(t) = t^{-3/2}sin⁡(π×t1/4)(t>1/2)\sin(\pi \times t^{1/4})(t>1/2) ϕk(t)=t−3/2\phi_k(t) = t^{-3/2}sin⁡(4π×k×t1/4)(t>1/2),k=1,2\sin(4\pi \times k \times t^{1/4})(t>1/2), k = 1,2 plot show simulated data looks like:  software performs adaptively-smoothed functional principal component analysis. main function package afpca:fpca.adapt. code illustrates : plots show estimated mean function functional principal components  plots shows two examples observed functions respective reconstructions.
code: library(afpca)  simulated_data <- simulate_adaptive_functional_data(N.subj = 20) afpca.output <- fpca.adapt(data = simulated_data)

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis

what: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: Decomposes functional observations using adaptive functional principal component analysis. method incorporates adaptive smoothness estimated decomposition using penalized likelihood framework

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
code: fpca.adapt(   data,   col_name = "name",   basis = "trunc.poly",   poly.degree = 2,   knots = NA,   nbs = 35,   n.comp = 18,   pve = 0.99,   normalize.scores = TRUE,   orthogonalize_scores = TRUE,   orthogonalize_fpcs = TRUE,   ntimes = 200,   convergence.thresh = 1e-04 )

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: data data frame, matrix, tidyfun "tfd.reg" object functional observations. col_name Column name data. Required Y tibble. package expects column "dft" object tidyfun basis Type spline basis. "os" B-spline basis "trunc.poly" truncated polynomials (default) poly.degree degree spline basis knots Number knots used generate spline basis estimate mean function functional principal components nbs Number spline basis generate mean function functional principal components n.comp prespecified number principal components pve proportion variance explained; used choose number functional principal components normalize.scores normalize scores iteration (default true, recommended) orthogonalize_scores orthogonalize scores iteration (default true, recommended) orthogonalize_fpcs orthogonalize FPCs iteration (default true, recommended) ntimes Maximum number iterations convergence.thresh Threshold convergence penalized likelihood

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: function returns object class "afpca" contains following: Y observed data. Pass either matrix, "dft" object tidyverse, tibble. tibble passed, "col_name" required Y_hat reconstructed fitted values mean vector estimated mean function fpcs tp (number timepoints) npc (number fpcs) matrix estimated eigenfunctions scores n (number subjects) npc (number fpcs) matrix estimated scores eigenvalues estimated eigenvalues .e. variances fpc scores Basis list containing penalized (Theta_beta) penalized (Theta_b) spline basis Theta concatenation (Theta_beta) penalized (Theta_b) spline basis coef estimated coefficients, coef %*% theta = fpcs convergence logical, algorithm converged .iter number iterations needed convergence

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: Paper

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
